sections.paragraph:
Arabic ذَارَ Med. Ye, to loathe. Intrans. to be loathsome. Job 19:17, רוּחִי זָרָה לְאִשְׁתִּי “my breath is loathsome to my wife.” Others, whom formerly I followed, take this according to the Syriac version, “my mind is (i.e. I am) become estranged from my wife.” Hence זָרָא (for זָרָה) loathing.

[In Thes. under זוּר No. II; see above.]
